element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Helicopter drop-off animation establishes vehicle control before combat
Always shoots upward; basic sustained-fire weapon
Directional weapon; destroys gates, buildings, and most vehicles in one shot
Collectible objectives; up to 8 per jeep; extraction at heliports grants points and weapon upgrades
Provide grenade/missile launcher upgrades; strategic routing incentive
Required to deliver prisoners; successive extractions grant additional upgrades
Three upgrade levels possible; longer reach, shrapnel spread (2-4 directions)
Can be run over; basic threat; first enemy type encountered
Destroy jeep on collision; require evasion or destruction
Stationary hazards; destroyed by grenade/missile launcher; spatial awareness required
NES-exclusive boss variant; projectile threat escalation
Horizontal arena; six turrets; requires sustained evasion and targeting
Final challenge; effectively countered by machine gun; highest threat level

element_introduction_order.element: Terrain Variety (Jungle, Water, Hill, Mountain, Base)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental context shift; no documented mechanical variance per source

typical_relief_triggers: Prisoner extraction at heliports (points awarded, weapon upgrade potential)
typical_tension_triggers: POW carrier capacity management (full jeep = forced extraction detour)
Missile launcher upgrade loss on vehicle loss
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation across six checkpoints; continuous level structure with repeated collect-extract cycles creates rhythmic tension-relief loops; boss encounters provide climactic tension peaks

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Solitary camps and scattered POWs encourage exploration off critical path; heliport proximity determines extraction efficiency

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction phase (Alpha): basic enemy types, single-hit grenade launcher, learning vehicle control and turret mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation phase (Baker-Delta): artillery turrets, upgraded missile launcher variants, increased enemy vehicle density, mid-tier bosses (statues, battleship)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Climax phase (Tango-Zulu): flamethrower tank boss, maximum prisoner capacity challenges, sustained high-threat enemy presence, extended level length (final stage doubled in NES version)
notable_spikes: Checkpoint Charlie/Delta: Introduction of missile-spitting statue bosses (NES-exclusive mechanic)
Checkpoint Delta: Battleship boss with six turrets represents mid-game peak difficulty
Checkpoint Zulu: Final boss (flamethrower tank) + prisoner camps combination + extended terrain

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divided into six named checkpoints (Alpha, Baker, Charlie, Delta, Tango, Zulu), each with distinct terrain and terminating boss. Replaces arcade's single continuous level.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Alpha (jungle opening level, helicopter drop animation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players navigate to prisoner camps, collect POWs (up to 8 per jeep), then route to heliports for extraction. Successive extractions grant weapon upgrades. Primary gameplay loop across all checkpoints.
NES version doubled stage width from 256px (FDS) to 512px, enabling horizontal scrolling and increased maneuverability. Affects level geometry and vehicle positioning strategies.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All NES stages vs. FDS equivalents

notable_level_patterns.description: Each checkpoint terminates with unique boss encounter (statues, battleship, flamethrower tank). Boss defeat required to advance. Bosses introduce NES-exclusive variants.
Colored gates (red in FDS, green in NES) block progression; destructible only by grenade/missile launcher. Creates navigation chokepoints and weapon upgrade dependencies.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Camp access gates across all levels

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Jackal NES/FDS documentation)
wikipedia (Jackal video game - gameplay and version differences)
